body:
### Describe the bug

I've book looking at how scipy.spatial.cKDTree lazy-initializes a cached property using the `py_safe` wrappers for the C++ `call_once`/`once_flag` APIs. While working on that, I observed data races in my first approach, which involved defining a closure and then calling it with `py_safe_call_object_once`:

```cython
cdef class cKDTree:
    cdef:
        py_safe_once_flag flag
        ....
    ....
    property tree:
        def __get__(cKDTree self):
            cdef cKDTreeNode n
            cdef ckdtree *cself = self.cself
            if self._python_tree is None:
                def closure():
                    n = cKDTreeNode()
                    n._setup(self, node=cself.ctree, level=0)
                    self._python_tree = n
                py_safe_call_object_once(self.flag, closure)
            return self._python_tree
```

Instead, I had to define a helper class with a `__call__` method:

```cython
cdef class call_once_wrapper:
    cdef:
        cKDTree tree

    def __cinit__(call_once_wrapper self, cKDTree tree):
        self.tree = tree

    def __call__(self):
        n = cKDTreeNode()
        n._setup(self.tree, node=self.tree.cself.ctree, level=0)
        self.tree._python_tree = n

cdef class cKDTree:
    cdef:
        py_safe_once_flag flag
        ....
    ....
    property tree:
        def __get__(cKDTree self):
            cdef cKDTreeNode n
            cdef ckdtree *cself = self.cself
            cdef call_once_wrapper wrapper
            if self._python_tree is None:
                wrapper = call_once_wrapper(self)
                py_safe_call_object_once(self.flag, wrapper)
            return self._python_tree
````

That doesn't race.

Unfortunately I don't have a simple example to trigger this outside SciPy ☹️.

Race report:

<details>


</details>

I uploaded `_ckdtree.cpp` here: https://gist.github.com/ngoldbaum/9e192ebf2ba2f0387ed23b5c1d5f5bab

### Code to reproduce the behaviour:

Apply the following diff to the [`thread-safe-kdtree`](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/compare/main...ngoldbaum:scipy:thread-safe-kdtree) branch on my fork of SciPy:

```diff
diff --git a/scipy/spatial/_ckdtree.pyx b/scipy/spatial/_ckdtree.pyx
index 13cc1f36f2..b68006d6c1 100644
--- a/scipy/spatial/_ckdtree.pyx
+++ b/scipy/spatial/_ckdtree.pyx
@@ -408,19 +408,6 @@ cdef np.intp_t get_num_workers(workers: object, kwargs: dict) except -1:
     return n
 
 
-cdef class call_once_wrapper:
-    cdef:
-        cKDTree tree
-
-    def __cinit__(call_once_wrapper self, cKDTree tree):
-        self.tree = tree
-
-    def __call__(self):
-        n = cKDTreeNode()
-        n._setup(self.tree, node=self.tree.cself.ctree, level=0)
-        self.tree._python_tree = n
-
-
 # Main cKDTree class
 # ==================
 
@@ -558,10 +545,12 @@ cdef class cKDTree:
         def __get__(cKDTree self):
             cdef cKDTreeNode n
             cdef ckdtree *cself = self.cself
-            cdef call_once_wrapper wrapper
             if self._python_tree is None:
-                wrapper = call_once_wrapper(self)
-                py_safe_call_object_once(self.flag, wrapper)
+                def closure():
+                    n = cKDTreeNode()
+                    n._setup(self, node=cself.ctree, level=0)
+                    self._python_tree = n
+                py_safe_call_object_once(self.flag, closure)
             return self._python_tree
 
     def __cinit__(cKDTree self):
```

Then build Python, NumPy, and SciPy with TSan instrumentation. The [cpython_sanity](https://github.com/nascheme/cpython_sanity) docker containers might be useful. Also see the manual build instructions here: https://github.com/Quansight-Labs/free-threaded-compatibility/issues/309.

I built SciPy itself with this command on my Mac with LLVM installed from homebrew:

```
LDFLAGS="-L/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/lib/c++ -L/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/lib/unwind -lunwind" CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ PKG_CONFIG_PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openblas/lib/pkgconfig" spin build -v --clean -- -Dbuildtype=debugoptimized -Dc_args=-fsanitize=thread -Dcpp_args=-fsanitize=thread -Dc_link_args=-fsanitize=thread -Dcpp_link_args=-fsanitize=thread -Dbuildtype=debugoptimized
```

And then you should be able to trigger the race and TSan report by doing:

```
spin test "scipy/spatial/tests/test_kdtree.py::test_multithreaded_tree_access" -- -svx
```

### Expected behaviour

No data races reported by TSan.

### OS

MacOS

### Python version

Python 3.15.0a7+ free-threading build (heads/main:cd52172, Mar 12 2026, 11:46:33) [Clang 21.1.7 ]

### Cython version

3.2.4

### Additional context

https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/24799

comment_data.body:
Is the race not:

```
            if self._python_tree is None:  # happens outside call once
```

and

```
                    self._python_tree = n  # happens inside call once
```

???

I think

```
        def __get__(cKDTree self):
            cdef cKDTreeNode n
            cdef ckdtree *cself = self.cself
            def closure():
                n = cKDTreeNode()
                n._setup(self, node=cself.ctree, level=0)
                self._python_tree = n
            py_safe_call_object_once(self.flag, closure)
            return self._python_tree
```

would work but would be expensive because you'd need to create the closure each time even if unused.
(Above comment is just from looking at the code rather than actually running it so could be wrong. I'm happy to investigate further)

Argh, you're right. Thanks for helping me understand what was going wrong!

I'm going to send in a documentation PR that adds an example to https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/userguide/freethreading.html#use-c-for-low-level-synchronization-primitives. It was surprisingly tricky to get this right!
